_provenance: Schema-derived from AC-P2.7.2. L1 selection metric: score(symphony) = sum_t(max(0, target[t] - sym.exposure[t])) + sum_t(max(0, sym.exposure[t] - target[t]) * OVER_SHOOT_PENALTY). Symphony with LOWEST score wins. OVER_SHOOT_PENALTY=1.0 (symmetric L1). Martello-Toth 1990 single-pick knapsack variant.
description: Three candidate symphonies; symphony B has the lowest L1 distance to the target_reduction profile.

derivations.sym-A_score: AAPL: max(0, 5000-2000)=3000 + max(0, 2000-5000)*1.0=0. MSFT: max(0, 3000-1000)=2000 + max(0, 1000-3000)*1.0=0. GOOG in sym-A but not in target: max(0, 0-8000)=0 (target has no GOOG so target[GOOG]=0) + max(0, 8000-0)*1.0=8000. WAIT: GOOG has no target entry — score += 8000*1.0. Total: 3000+2000+8000=13000. NOTE: fixture recalculated; sym-A exposure includes GOOG which has target=0 -> overshoot=8000. Corrected score=13000.
derivations.winner: sym-B with score=300 is clearly minimum
